 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","title":"Demo Chrome: tabs/palette/help/status overlays fully mouse interactive","description":"Background
- The demo chrome renders the tab bar, status bar, help overlay, and command palette.
- Tab bar hit regions exist and tab switching works, but most other chrome is keyboard-only.

Goal
- Make the demo's chrome and overlays fully mouse-clickable and scrollable in the obvious way.

Scope / Work Items
- Tab bar:
  - Hover highlight, click-to-switch (already exists), and optional scroll-wheel-to-cycle.
- Category tabs (if rendered):
  - Click category pill to jump.
- Command palette:
  - Hover highlight; click selects; double click executes; wheel scrolls list.
- Help overlay:
  - Clickable close button; wheel scroll if content overflows.
- Status bar:
  - Clickable affordances for the most common toggles (help, palette, a11y, perf HUD, debug) without ambiguity.

Acceptance Criteria
- Users can navigate the entire demo UI with mouse alone (except text input entry).
- Mouse interactions are logged when demo logging is enabled.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for hit-id mapping for chrome elements.
- Snapshot tests for hover states + palette selection.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/chrome.rs`
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.005628799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:02:27.444727423Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:02:12.010534630Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chrome","command-palette","demo-showcase","mouse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.005628799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.544822323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":840,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedSnow (claude-code, opus-4.6) claiming. This follows directly from bd-iuvb.17.2 (my dispatcher work). Will register hit regions in chrome rendering for overlays, status bar toggles, and palette; wire up the dispatcher priority chain.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:26:24Z"},{"id":841,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon claiming. Tab bar and category tabs already register hit regions. Status bar toggles and overlays have hit ID constants defined but NO hit regions are registered during rendering. Will add register_hit_region calls to render_status_bar and overlay renderers.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:26:28Z"},{"id":843,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon: Hit regions registered for all chrome elements: status bar toggles, help overlay, a11y panel, tour overlay, perf HUD, debug, evidence. Tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:58:01Z"},{"id":844,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RedSnow closing. All chrome elements now fully mouse-interactive with hit regions and 11 unit tests. Palette item-level interaction deferred to bd-iuvb.17.11.7.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:02:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","title":"Demo Shortcuts: global key standards + per-screen interactive keybindings","description":"Background
- The demo already supports a help overlay and per-screen keybinding lists.
- Many screens still feel passive; adding meaningful shortcuts is the easiest way to make the demo feel like a 'lab' rather than a gallery.

Goal
- Establish a consistent global shortcut vocabulary and expand per-screen shortcuts so each screen is discoverably interactive.

Global Shortcut Standards (proposed)
- `?` toggle help overlay.
- `Ctrl+K` command palette.
- `Ctrl+P` performance HUD.
- `Ctrl+I` inspector / hit regions overlay (if present).
- `M` toggle mouse mode (see mouse policy task).
- `Esc` closes the topmost overlay (palette/help/modal/tour).

Per-Screen Requirements
- Each screen must have at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts that change state or demonstrate a capability.
- Every shortcut must appear in help overlay under the active screen.

Acceptance Criteria
- Shortcut set is coherent across screens (same key means same kind of action).
- No conflicts with text input screens (respect focus).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for help overlay including global bindings + screen bindings.
- Snapshot tests for help overlay at 80x24 and 120x40 for at least 3 representative screens.","notes":"Claiming: will consolidate global shortcut vocabulary + ensure help overlay stays in sync; start by auditing current global keys and adding regression tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentLantern","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:12.562979642Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","help","shortcuts","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.313685570Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":846,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FoggyFalcon contributed 13 integration tests for global keyboard shortcuts (d4ecb564): m/F6 mouse capture, Shift+A a11y, F12 debug, Ctrl+P perf HUD, Ctrl+K palette, ? help toggle, Shift+H/L vim navigation, Esc overlay dismiss, shortcut isolation, toggle idempotence.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:41Z"},{"id":848,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 7 more Esc priority and overlay isolation tests (commit 6d0a8e39): esc_does_not_close_help, esc_does_not_close_debug, esc_does_not_close_perf_hud, esc_priority_palette_before_a11y (verifies 2-stage dismiss), esc_with_help_and_a11y_closes_only_a11y, shortcuts_ignored_while_palette_is_open, number_keys_navigate_to_screens. Total shortcut tests: 20. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:35:23Z"},{"id":850,"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 5 more shortcut tests (commit 846945d0): tab_cycles_screens_forward, backtab_cycles_screens_backward, ctrl_t_cycles_theme, q_key_is_quit_signal (isolation check), multiple_overlays_coexist (help+debug+perf HUD can be on simultaneously). Total: 25 global shortcut tests. —FoggyFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:42:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","title":"Guided Tour v2: add missing best-of moments + mouse/shortcut narration","description":"Background
- Guided tour mode exists and is deterministic, but the storyboard is currently not showcasing some of the most compelling screens and interactions.

Goal
- Expand and refine the guided tour to highlight the highest-signal demo features, with explicit narration of mouse/keyboard interactions.

Scope / Work Items
- Storyboard updates:
  - Ensure tour covers: Inline mode story, determinism lab, time-travel/snapshots, hit testing/hyperlinks, performance HUD/budgeting, and at least one 'big visual' (VFX/Quake).
  - Include at least 2 'interactive moments' where the tour explicitly demonstrates a click/drag/scroll or keypress sequence.
- Overlay copy updates:
  - Callouts should point out the exact interaction ('Click the tab', 'Scroll here', 'Press Ctrl+K').
  - Keep copy tight and high-signal.

Constraints
- Tour remains deterministic under fixed seed and time-freeze.
- Tour must not depend on real wall-clock delays.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tour completes in ~2-3 minutes and feels like a cinematic product tour.
- Tour demonstrates mouse and shortcuts explicitly.
- Tour does not break inline scrollback story.

Tests / Validation
- Update existing tour E2E harness to cover new steps and assert stable step ordering/log output.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.512011698Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","guided-tour","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.623294028Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.439885192Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.203082627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:27.511911070Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.10.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.979851049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.383701981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.11.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.677897754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.893529658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.286884579Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.13.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:26.592109121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.144673588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:56.841142653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:24.477825496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:24.778447577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.6","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:52:25.073821838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","title":"Testing: mouse/shortcuts regression harness (unit + snapshot + PTY)","description":"Background
- Adding mouse routing and lots of shortcuts risks subtle regressions (overlay priority, focus bugs, scrollback theft, nondeterminism).

Goal
- Add a focused, deterministic test suite that locks in the new interaction behavior.

Scope / Work Items
- Unit tests (fast):
  - Mouse dispatch priority (palette > help > tour > chrome > screen).
  - Click activation edge consistency (down vs up).
  - Hover coalescing and drag lifecycle.
- Snapshot tests:
  - At least one snapshot per interaction category (palette hover, list click selection, scrollbar drag).
- PTY / E2E tests:
  - Send real SGR mouse sequences through PTY to validate parsing + routing.
  - Inline-mode run asserts scrollback-preserving defaults (mouse capture disabled unless toggled).

Acceptance Criteria
- Interaction behavior is deterministic and regression-protected.
- Tests are stable across sizes (80x24 and 120x40).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.927421830Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","e2e","mouse","shortcuts","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.746047285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.036084728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.333059545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.635703478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.927366547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Adding mouse routing and lots of shortcuts risks subtle regressions (overlay priority, focus bugs, scrollback theft, nondeterminism).

Goal
- Add a focused, deterministic test suite that locks in the new interaction behavior.

Scope / Work Items
- Unit tests (fast):
  - Mouse dispatch priority (palette > help > tour > chrome > screen).
  - Click activation edge consistency (down vs up).
  - Hover coalescing and drag lifecycle.
- Snapshot tests:

Acceptance Criteria
- Interaction behavior is deterministic and regression-protected.
- Tests are stable across sizes (80x24 and 120x40).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderHollow","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:53:08.823285496Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","e2e","mouse","shortcuts","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:41.940861871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:57.746047285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.036084728Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.333059545Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.635703478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:44:58.927366547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","title":"Mouse/Shortcuts Pass: Tour screens","description":"Purpose
- Track and drive the mouse-first + shortcut-rich upgrades for the demo's Tour-category experiences.

Included Screens (current)
- Guided Tour (base exists; v2 work tracked elsewhere in bd-iuvb.17.6)
- Dashboard
- Inline Mode Story (scrollback-first constraints)

Interaction Goals (Tour)
- Mouse-first navigation and 'clickable tiles' where the UI suggests it.
- Clear, cinematic affordances: hover highlight, click to drill in, wheel scroll where content overflows.
- Shortcuts that demonstrate capabilities quickly (toggle overlays, switch tiles, trigger scripted sequences).

Definition of Done
- Each included screen has at least 1 meaningful mouse interaction and at least 3-5 meaningful shortcuts.
- Help overlay entries are accurate and tested.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"HazyRaven","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:34:20.734837655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts","tour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:45:48.169248562Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","title":"Dashboard: clickable tiles + mouse routing + power shortcuts","description":"Background
- Dashboard is the demo's cinematic overview. It already lays out multiple 'tiles' that visually read as clickable panes.
- The dashboard view currently registers pane hit regions for several tiles (see `dashboard.rs` registering `chrome::register_pane_hit`), but global mouse routing does not yet use those hits.

Goal
- Make the dashboard feel like a real clickable home screen.

Mouse Interactions
- Hover highlights the tile under the pointer.
- Click a tile to navigate to the corresponding screen.
- Scroll wheel over a tile that has scrollable content scrolls that tile (or shows a hint that it is not scrollable).

Keyboard Shortcuts (examples)
- `Enter`: activate currently highlighted tile.
- `1-9`: jump to specific tiles in a stable order (documented).
- `R`: refresh/reseed any animated tiles deterministically (if used).

Acceptance Criteria
- Clicking any dashboard tile navigates deterministically and stops the guided tour if active.
- Help overlay lists dashboard shortcuts.
- Snapshot test covers hover highlight + active tile.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/dashboard.rs` (tile layout + pane hit registration)
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` (mouse routing)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkRidge","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:13:00.386710034Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo-showcase","mouse","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:01.456529033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","title":"Inline Mode Story: mouse-friendly without scrollback regression","description":"Background
- Inline Mode is a core differentiator: preserve native terminal scrollback while keeping chrome stable.
- Mouse capture can easily break this by stealing the scroll wheel; this screen must be the 'golden example' of doing it right.

Goal
- Make the Inline Mode story screen interactive and mouse-friendly *while keeping scrollback preservation obvious and intact*.

Mouse Interactions
- Default (mouse mode OFF): wheel scrolls terminal scrollback; screen should explicitly tell the user that this is intentional.
- Clickable elements inside the inline UI:
  - Click on highlighted callouts to jump to the next/previous story beat.
  - Click on 'Try it' prompts to trigger a deterministic mini-action (e.g., show/hide chrome, toggle a marker line).
- Optional opt-in: allow toggling mouse mode ON for this screen with explicit warning that wheel now scrolls UI.

Keyboard Shortcuts
- Keys to step the story forward/back, toggle example modes, and toggle mouse mode.
- Include a one-key 'reset story' action that restores the initial state deterministically.

Acceptance Criteria
- Inline scrollback is preserved by default (wheel scrolls terminal scrollback).
- Any opt-in mouse mode change is explicit and discoverable.
- Screen is still demonstrably interactive (click + shortcuts) even in default scrollback-preserving mode.
- Help overlay accurately describes the interaction contract and tradeoffs.

Tests / Validation
- PTY E2E: Inline mode run asserts mouse capture is not enabled by default.
- Snapshot test covers the explanatory copy and the interactive affordances.

Key References
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/inline_mode_story.rs`
- `crates/ftui-core/src/terminal_session.rs`","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","inline-mode","mouse","scrollback","shortcuts"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb.17.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T01:46:29.588738099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-zc0d7","title":"Perf: structured logging for tests/e2e","description":"Goal

Acceptance
- Logs are machine-validated in tests with clear failure output.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:35.807044451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:39:05.752889755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:39:05.752871010Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","perf","subtask"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zc0d7","depends_on_id":"bd-3gtf7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:46.240820336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":745,"issue_id":"bd-zc0d7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"JSONL structured logging complete. emit_metrics_jsonl() emits all metrics fields (schema_version, event, seq, run_id, seed, screen_mode, sample, layout_mode, tier, glyph_mode, wrap_mode, render_epoch, parse_ms, layout_ms, render_ms, layout_iterations, objective_score, constraint_violations, bends, symmetry, compactness, edge_length_variance, label_collisions, fallback_tier, fallback_reason). Two machine-validation tests: metrics_jsonl_line_includes_required_fields (validates all field types via serde_json) and metrics_jsonl_quality_fields_populated (validates quality fields are non-null for valid samples). All 86 mermaid_showcase tests pass, clippy clean. Agent: BrightSnow","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:39:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zdbm","title":"PTY integration test: TerminalSession cleanup sequences","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:22:51.955647053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:25:12.656720918Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:25:12.656701993Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-zj0es","title":"State: viewport transform behavior","description":"Goal
- Define zoom/fit/pan behavior AND viewport size override behavior.

Deliverables
- Zoom in/out step, reset, fit-to-viewport logic.
- Viewport size override controls (width/height) with clamping.
- Pan offsets clamped to bounds if applicable.

Acceptance
- Viewport behavior is deterministic and consistent across sizes.

 {"id":"bd-zlq9","title":"Add momentum scrolling with velocity tracking","description":"# Add momentum scrolling with velocity tracking

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Smooth scrolling requires velocity tracking and momentum continuation.

## Technical Design

```rust
impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    /// Scroll by delta (positive = down)
    pub fn scroll(&mut self, delta: i32) {
        let new_offset = (self.scroll_offset as i32 + delta)
            .max(0)
            .min(self.len().saturating_sub(1) as i32);
        self.scroll_offset = new_offset as usize;
        
        // Cancel follow mode if scrolling up
        if delta < 0 {
            self.follow_mode = false;
        }
    }
    
    /// Scroll to specific item
    pub fn scroll_to(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.scroll_offset = idx.min(self.len().saturating_sub(1));
        self.follow_mode = false;
    }
    
    /// Scroll to bottom (enable follow mode)
    pub fn scroll_to_bottom(&mut self) {
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.scroll_offset = self.len() - self.visible_count;
        }
    }
    
    /// Start momentum scroll with initial velocity
    pub fn fling(&mut self, velocity: f32) {
        self.scroll_velocity = velocity;
        if velocity > 0.0 {
            self.follow_mode = false;
        }
    }
    
    /// Update momentum (call each frame)
    pub fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        if self.scroll_velocity.abs() > 0.5 {
            // Apply velocity
            let delta = (self.scroll_velocity * dt.as_secs_f32() * 60.0) as i32;
            self.scroll(delta);
            
            // Apply friction
            self.scroll_velocity *= 0.92;
        } else {
            self.scroll_velocity = 0.0;
        }
    }
    
    /// Enable/disable follow mode
    pub fn set_follow(&mut self, follow: bool) {
        self.follow_mode = follow;
        if follow {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    /// Check if at bottom
    pub fn is_at_bottom(&self) -> bool {
        self.scroll_offset + self.visible_count >= self.len()
    }
    
    /// Called when items are added
    pub fn on_items_added(&mut self, count: usize) {
        // Update external len if needed
        if let VirtualizedStorage::External { len, .. } = &mut self.items {
            *len += count;
        }
        
        if self.follow_mode {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    /// Page up/down
    pub fn page_up(&mut self) {
        self.scroll(-(self.visible_count as i32));
    }
    
    pub fn page_down(&mut self) {
        self.scroll(self.visible_count as i32);
    }
    
    /// Home/End
    pub fn scroll_to_start(&mut self) {
        self.scroll_offset = 0;
        self.follow_mode = false;
    }
    
    pub fn scroll_to_end(&mut self) {
        self.scroll_to_bottom();
        self.follow_mode = true;
    }
}

/// Scroll input handling
impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    pub fn handle_scroll_event(&mut self, event: &Event) {
        match event {
            Event::Mouse(MouseEvent::ScrollUp { .. }) => self.scroll(-3),
            Event::Mouse(MouseEvent::ScrollDown { .. }) => self.scroll(3),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Up, _) => self.scroll(-1),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Down, _) => self.scroll(1),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::PageUp, _) => self.page_up(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::PageDown, _) => self.page_down(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Home, _) => self.scroll_to_start(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::End, _) => self.scroll_to_end(),
            _ => {}
        }
    }
}
```

## Momentum Physics
- Velocity in items/second
- Friction coefficient: 0.92 per frame
- Threshold: stop when |velocity| < 0.5
- Smooth deceleration over ~20 frames

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scroll by delta works
- [ ] Page up/down work
- [ ] Home/End work
- [ ] Momentum scrolling smooth
- [ ] Follow mode auto-scrolls on add
- [ ] Manual scroll cancels follow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:41.092101550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:46:29.143532773Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:46:29.143444948Z","close_reason":"Momentum scrolling with velocity tracking implemented and tested","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zlq9","depends_on_id":"bd-14s3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
